Dashboard Archive: 2026-03-30 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
CLMT dominates today's ranking at #1 with a composite score of 94.4, driven by exceptional returns across all timeframes (9.3% in 1d, 160.52% in 1y) and flawless positioning above all major moving averages, but its momentum_signal of 2.85 flags it as "overextended," suggesting potential pullback risk. NTR ranks second with more modest but consistent gains (1.19% 1d, 50.39% 1y) and notably passes 4 out of 5 ratio consistency checks, though its bad_spy_score of only 0.1677 indicates it struggled relative to SPY during market downturns, unlike CLMT's resilient 0.9864 score. The sharp contrast between these two top performers illustrates a key tension: CLMT shows stronger absolute momentum and defensive characteristics during SPY stress, while NTR demonstrates ratio consistency but with less reliable outperformance in adverse conditions. Both stocks clear all SMA gates and show positive relative returns versus SPY and QQQ across multiple horizons, but CLMT's extreme ratio spreads (particularly 2.6 for 3m/1m and 2.06 for 1y/3m) coupled with its overextended flag suggest caution on entry timing.
For educational and informational purposes only. Not financial advice. Past performance does not guarantee future results.
